answers.value: Riverside Station Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented Development Project
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: Riverside Station Partners, LLC (Attn: Jonathan K. Meyers, Managing Member)
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: Northwest corner of Riverside Boulevard and 35th Avenue, City of Sacramento, Sacramento County, California (APNs 031-0450-012, -013, -014)
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value:
The Riverside Station Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented Development Project proposes redevelopment of approximately 6.8 acres of underutilized commercial and surface parking lots into a higher-density, mixed-use, transit-supportive community adjacent to an existing light rail station. The project includes construction of four primary buildings ranging from 4 to 7 stories in height, with a total of approximately 410 multi-family residential units (a mix of studios, one-bedroom, two-bedroom, and three-bedroom units), 32,000 square feet of ground-floor neighborhood-serving commercial/retail space, and approximately 18,000 square feet of office/co-working space oriented toward small businesses and remote workers.

The project also includes structured parking (approximately 420 parking spaces) in podium and wrapped configurations, secure bicycle storage for residents and visitors (approximately 260 long-term and 80 short-term bicycle parking spaces), enhanced pedestrian connections to the adjacent light rail station and bus stops, and off-site improvements such as new sidewalks, ADA-compliant curb ramps, upgraded crosswalks, and traffic signal modifications at the Riverside Boulevard/35th Avenue intersection.

Site amenities include publicly accessible open space plazas, a linear greenway along the eastern property boundary with native landscaping and shade trees, rooftop terraces, community rooms, and on-site stormwater treatment features (bioretention planters and underground detention) designed to meet current City and State low-impact development (LID) standards. The project involves demolition of approximately 42,000 square feet of existing single-story commercial buildings and removal of existing surface parking lots, followed by site grading, utility relocation and upgrades (water, sewer, storm drain, electrical, and telecommunications), and phased construction over an anticipated 30- to 36-month build-out period.

The project requires a Rezone from General Commercial (C-2) to General Commercial with a Transit-Oriented Development Overlay (C-2-TO), a Planned Unit Development (PUD) Establishment and Guidelines, a Tentative Map for subdivision into multiple parcels, Design Review, and associated approvals and agreements with the City of Sacramento and the Sacramento Regional Transit District for access and frontage improvements.
